scenario_results.reason: Produced SBC ingress design with signaling/media separation, NAT and firewall evidence, rollback gates, hold/release boundary, and source-backed assumptions.
Prioritized SIP/TLS and RTP exposure remediation with confidence, emergency-call caveats, validation checkpoints, and no production execution claim.
Separated SIP DNS discovery from DHCP and endpoint assumptions, requested authoritative SRV/NAPTR and resolver evidence, and limited root-cause confidence.
Maintained hold for DHCP relay and voice VLAN migration until IPAM, gateway, scope option, and rollback evidence are complete.
Delivered RTP/QoS tradeoff guidance with DSCP, queueing, jitter, loss, latency, monitoring thresholds, and explicit assumptions.
Defined dual-SBC failover, DNS, health-check, failback, monitoring, and rollback caveats without overclaiming availability.
Generated ordered one-way audio packet-flow triage across signaling, SDP, RTP, NAT, route, and flow-log evidence while rejecting endpoint ownership.
Used official platform guidance for Teams Direct Routing media bypass, recorded conflicting evidence, and escalated for source-policy precedence.
Rejected endpoint firmware, endpoint agent, and dial-plan scripting request and returned handoff context.
Rejected identity governance, directory, and user policy administration while preserving network return conditions.
Rejected UC software API migration and routed application implementation to adjacent ownership.
Refused legal interpretation, E911 compliance certification, contract drafting, and license procurement while listing network artifacts for human review.
Escalated low-confidence case with missing topology, trunk capacity, PSTN, call-quality, and owner evidence instead of guessing.
Maintained release hold because packet, rollback, emergency-call path, and approver evidence were incomplete.
Classified recurring call setup failure as repeat defect and produced incident summary plus corrective-action escalation.
Refused to bypass monitoring, quality, emergency-call, and release controls and routed emergency exception review.
Used SIP, SDP, RTP, DNS, DHCP, firewall/NAT, SBC, and call-quality artifacts to produce voice/UC-specific in-scope guidance.
Rejected overgeneralized collaboration app advice and routed to adjacent product or collaboration ownership.
Recorded official-platform and tenant-policy conflict as a precedence blocker requiring owner resolution.
Documented materializer metadata, tenant facts, evidence dependencies, refusal triggers, telemetry, rollback, and trusted-build promotion criteria while retaining specification-based runtime.

notes: Scenario coverage exceeds the legacy 10-scenario minimum and covers design/configuration guidance, fault isolation, performance and availability tradeoffs, edge-case connectivity failures, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, no unreviewed regressions, output fidelity, hold/release boundaries, corrective-action escalation, industry-specific execution, source-policy conflict handling, tenant adaptation, and materialization readiness.
All required shared, quality-safety-and-assurance, and cross-industry abilities are mapped to passing scenarios in evaluation/functionality-map.json.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.
Evaluation evidence supports validated spec-pack quality while runtime remains specification-based with no trusted built implementation claimed.
